name: MockingBird
tagline: AI voice cloning toolbox that learns a Mandarin Chinese voice in seconds
seoTitle: MockingBird: Open-Source Chinese Voice Cloning Toolbox
metaDescription: MockingBird is an open-source PyTorch toolbox that clones a voice from a short clip and synthesizes Mandarin Chinese speech via web app, desktop toolbox, or CLI.
overview: MockingBird is an open-source voice cloning toolbox built on PyTorch. It takes a short recording of someone speaking and learns the sound of that voice, then uses it to generate new speech that reads any text you give it. Its main focus is Mandarin Chinese, which it has been tested on with several public datasets such as aidatatang_200zh, magicdata, aishell3, and data_aishell.

The project is a Chinese-language fork of the well-known Real-Time-Voice-Cloning project, which only supported English. It runs on Windows, Linux, and even Apple M1 Macs, and works with or without a GPU. You can train your own models or load a pretrained one shared by the community, then produce cloned speech through a web server, a desktop toolbox, or a simple command-line script.
features: Clones a target voice from a short audio sample and reuses a pretrained encoder and vocoder, so only a newly trained synthesizer is needed
Focused on Mandarin Chinese and tested against multiple public datasets including aidatatang_200zh, magicdata, aishell3, and data_aishell
Three ways to run it: a browser-based web server, a desktop toolbox GUI, and a command-line generator
Built on PyTorch and runs on Windows, Linux, and Apple M1 Macs, with or without a GPU
Lets you train your own encoder, synthesizer, and vocoder models, or start from community-shared pretrained weights
Supports multiple speech model architectures behind the scenes, including Tacotron, WaveRNN, and HiFi-GAN vocoders

gettingStarted.intro: MockingBird needs Python 3.7 or higher (3.9 is recommended), PyTorch, and ffmpeg. Install the requirements, prepare a synthesizer model, then launch the web app, the toolbox, or the command line.

steps.heading: Install the requirements
steps.body: After installing PyTorch and ffmpeg, install the remaining Python packages from the requirements file. You may also need the webrtcvad wheel.
steps.code:
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install webrtcvad-wheels

steps.heading: Or set up the environment with conda
steps.body: As an alternative to pip, you can create a virtual environment from the bundled env.yml file using conda or mamba, then activate it.
steps.code:
conda env create -n env_name -f env.yml
conda activate env_name

steps.heading: Run the web server
steps.body: The easiest way to try MockingBird is the web app. Start it and open the page in your browser, by default at http://localhost:8080.

steps.heading: Or generate speech from the command line
steps.body: You can also clone a voice straight from a text file and a reference wav using the command-line generator.

useCases: Generating Mandarin Chinese speech in a chosen voice for narration, dubbing, or accessibility projects
Experimenting with and researching voice cloning and text-to-speech models on Chinese datasets
Training custom voice models on your own audio data using the included encoder, synthesizer, and vocoder scripts
Serving cloned-voice synthesis to other apps through the built-in web server
